title: SSC MTS 2026
meta_description: SSC MTS 2026 — Multi Tasking Staff exam for 10th pass candidates. Sep-Nov 2026 exam dates, salary ₹18-22K/month, Physical Efficiency Test, Group C posts.

hero.one_liner: Government jobs for 10th pass — Multi Tasking Staff (Group C) posts in central government ministries, offices, and departments across India

what_is_it: SSC MTS (Multi Tasking Staff) is one of the easiest government exams to crack, requiring only 10th class pass qualification. It recruits for Group C non-gazetted, non-ministerial posts in various central government ministries, departments, and offices. MTS staff handle tasks like physical maintenance of records, general cleanliness, carrying files, photocopying, and other support functions in government offices.

The exam is conducted annually by SSC with typically 8,000-10,000 vacancies. The selection process has a single Computer Based Test (CBT) followed by a Physical Efficiency Test (PET) and Document Verification. The exam is bilingual (English + Hindi) and relatively straightforward — testing basic General Intelligence, Numerical Aptitude, General English, and General Awareness at the 10th class level.

Starting salary is at Pay Level 1 (₹18,000-56,900 basic pay scale). With Dearness Allowance and other allowances, the in-hand salary is approximately ₹18,000-22,000 per month. While this is the lowest pay scale in central government, MTS employees get all government benefits — job security, medical insurance, LTC, children's education allowance, pension (under NPS), and paid leaves.

For 2026, per the SSC Calendar 2026-27, the notification is expected on 30 June 2026 with the exam scheduled for September-November 2026. This makes it one of the later SSC exams in the calendar year.

key_details.label: Application Fee
key_details.value: ₹100 (Exempted for Women, SC, ST, Ex-Servicemen, PwBD)

key_details.label: Selection
key_details.value: Computer Based Test → Physical Efficiency Test (PET) → Document Verification

key_details.label: Posts
key_details.value: Peon, Daftary, Jamadar, Junior Gestetner Operator, Farash, Chowkidar in central govt offices

key_details.label: Posting
key_details.value: Central government offices across India — can request home state but no guarantee

exam_pattern.description: Single paper with 4 sections. No sectional time limit — manage your 90 minutes across all sections. Questions are at 10th class level difficulty. This is the main exam — your CBT score determines merit ranking.
exam_pattern.negative_marking: 0.75 marks per wrong answer (questions carry 3 marks each, penalty is 25%)

major_posts.department: Central Board of Indirect Taxes & Customs

extra_sections.heading: SSC MTS vs SSC CHSL — Which Should You Target?
rows: Can promote to LDC via exam

extra_sections.heading: What Does an MTS Employee Actually Do?
content: The 'Multi Tasking' in MTS means you perform various support tasks in a government office. Daily work typically includes:
Physical record management — carrying files between sections, arranging and maintaining old records in record rooms, binding and labeling files.
Office maintenance — general cleanliness of the office section, arranging furniture, managing stationery supplies, operating photocopier/printer.
Support duties — serving water/tea during meetings, carrying messages between officers, managing the dispatch section, answering basic phone calls.

Some MTS posts in specific departments have different duties: CBIC Havaldar involves manning entry gates, checking passes, and security-related tasks. MTS in post offices may involve mail sorting and delivery assistance.
Important reality check: MTS is the lowest rung in central government hierarchy. The work is manual/physical in nature.

However, it comes with permanent government job security, all benefits, and the opportunity to appear for departmental exams (like LDC exam) to get promoted to higher clerical posts. Many MTS employees have moved to LDC and UDC positions within 3-5 years through departmental promotions.

extra_sections.heading: Physical Efficiency Test (PET) — Requirements
content: After clearing the CBT, candidates must pass a Physical Efficiency Test. This is qualifying — not merit-based.
You just need to complete the tasks within the given time:
Males: Walk 1.6 km in 15 minutes, Cycle 8 km in 25 minutes.
Females: Walk 1 km in 20 minutes, Cycle 3 km in 25 minutes.
PwBD candidates are exempted from PET.
These are very basic physical fitness requirements — not athletic standards. Most young, reasonably fit candidates clear this easily.
Practice by walking/cycling regularly in the weeks before PET. Wear comfortable shoes and sports clothing on PET day.
Proper sleep and nutrition in the week before PET matter more than last-minute training.

preparation_tips: The entire exam is at 10th class difficulty level. Revise your Class 9-10 Math (percentage, ratio, average, profit-loss, geometry basics), Science (physics, chemistry, biology basics), and Social Studies (history, geography, civics).

For General English, focus on basic grammar — tenses, articles, prepositions, one-word substitutions, synonyms/antonyms, and sentence correction. Read 10th class English textbook if your English is weak. NCERT Class 10 English is perfect preparation.
Previous year papers are the BEST resource for SSC MTS. The patterns repeat heavily. Solve 20+ previous year papers and you'll see 40-50% similar questions in the actual exam.

General Awareness covers static GK (capitals, currencies, important days, first in India, Indian history basics) + last 6 months current affairs. One monthly current affairs magazine is sufficient. Follow The Hindu for daily news.

Time management is easier in MTS than CGL/CHSL — you have 54 seconds per question (vs 36 seconds in CGL). Use this advantage. Don't rush — focus on accuracy since the negative marking penalty (25%) is significant.
Practice physical efficiency: start walking/cycling 2-3 weeks before PET. The distances are short — even unfit candidates can clear with basic training.

faqs.question: Is MTS a permanent government job?
faqs.answer: Yes. SSC MTS is a permanent, pensionable central government job. You get all benefits — job security, medical insurance, LTC, children's education allowance, pension (under NPS), and paid leaves. It's not contractual or temporary.

faqs.question: Can I get promoted from MTS?
faqs.answer: Yes. MTS employees can appear for departmental exams (like the LDC limited departmental exam) to get promoted to Lower Division Clerk (Level 2). From LDC, promotions to UDC, Assistant, and higher posts are possible. Promotion from MTS to LDC typically takes 3-5 years of service eligibility.

faqs.question: MTS salary seems low. Is it worth it?
faqs.answer: ₹18,000-22,000/month may seem low, but consider: (1) It's a permanent govt job with zero risk of layoff, (2) Medical insurance for entire family, (3) Pension after retirement (NPS), (4) Annual increments + DA revisions increase salary over time, (5) Promotion opportunities exist, (6) In many tier-3 cities, ₹20K is a decent salary. For someone with only 10th pass qualification, MTS is one of the best career options available.

faqs.question: I have a graduation degree. Should I apply for MTS?
faqs.answer: You CAN apply (over-qualification is allowed), but you SHOULD focus on CGL (for graduates) and CHSL (for 12th pass) which offer much higher posts and salaries. Use MTS only as a backup safety net. Many graduates apply for MTS as insurance while primarily targeting CGL/CHSL.

faqs.question: Is there typing test in MTS?
faqs.answer: No. There is no typing test, skill test, or computer proficiency test in SSC MTS. The selection is based purely on CBT score + PET (qualifying). This makes MTS accessible to candidates who may not have typing or computer skills.

faqs.question: What happens after getting selected as MTS?
faqs.answer: After selection, you go through Document Verification (verify your original documents), Medical Examination (health check), and Training. The training period is typically 2-4 weeks at a training center where you learn office procedures and rules. After training, you're posted to a central government office in your preferred region (subject to vacancy and suitability).

image_suggestions: 10th class Math and English revision notes thumbnail

syllabus.subject: Numerical Aptitude
syllabus.topics: Number system, percentage, ratio, profit & loss, average, SI/CI, time & work, time & distance, basic geometry, data interpretation (simple)

syllabus.subject: Reasoning & General Intelligence
syllabus.topics: Analogy, classification, series, coding-decoding, direction, blood relations, Venn diagram, embedded figures, mirror/water image, paper folding, missing numbers

syllabus.subject: English Language
syllabus.topics: Spot the error, fill in blanks, synonym/antonym, spelling, idioms, sentence improvement, one word substitution, comprehension

syllabus.subject: General Awareness
syllabus.topics: Current affairs, Indian history, geography, polity, science, economics, sports, awards, books & authors, important dates

study_plan.tasks: SSC MTS is 10th-level. Focus on basic maths — percentage, ratio, average, time & work. Start Lucent GK. English vocabulary 15 words/day. Reasoning basics from R.S. Aggarwal.
Solve last 5 years SSC MTS papers. One mock test per week. Focus on speed — 25 questions in 45 minutes per section. Current affairs of last 6 months.
Daily mock tests. Revise GK notes. Focus on accuracy — negative marking is 0.25. Target: 80+ out of 100 for safe selection.

quiz.question: If 15% of a number is 45, what is the number?
Select the odd one out: Rose, Lily, Dog, Sunflower
